entry.title: PMDG Releases High Detail Update for the 737-900
entry.summary: Sim: FS24. PMDG has recently issued an update to their Boeing 737-900, bringing it up to the new MSFS 2024 standards through the “high detail update”, already available on the -700 and -600, greatly improving the flight deck, cabin, and externals. The “high detail” update substantially improves the aircraft’s model, adding high-detail tweaks to the operating access panels, ground equipment, landing gear bays, lighting, textures, and more. They have also rolled out small updates to the -600 and -700 to keep all of them on par as far as the “high detail” improvements go. ​ On another note, the Operations Center 2.0 will reach end of life in late May, not impacting MSFS users at all, but FSX and P3D users will have to manually back up the liveries they wish to keep, since they will no longer be available. They are putting great effort into WASM crash mitigation, and it is currently their number 1 strategic objective for the development team. There’s an update undergoing testing that is the first of maybe two or three updates specifically targeted toward reducing WASM crashes. ​ The add-on is available on their store for $49.99. If you enjoy our content and want to support Threshold, consider joining us on Patreon! ‍

entry.title: iniBuilds Updates Liverpool Airport for MSFS 2024
entry.summary: Sim: FS24. iniBuilds has recently issued an update to their Liverpool Airport (EGGP) for Microsoft Flight Simulator 2024, focusing on improving the scenery’s overall performance through lighting optimization, better LODding, and the use of sim props, among other tweaks. ​ Update v1.0.4 - the fourth since its release - brings a significant performance improvement to the terminal area, with reduced light count, improved LODs, and the implementation of sim props. Furthermore, it adds ground clutter, vegetation, and airfield details, while addressing lighting issues and environmental fixes around the terminal and taxiways. The complete changelog follows below: v1.0.4 - PERFORMANCE update to Terminal (Reduced lights, Implemented Sim Props, Improved LODs) - FIXED lights flickering/disappearing in multiple aircraft including Fenix A320 - ADDED GSE Clutter outside hangars / block 32R - ADDED airfield barriers at edge of Gate 10 - FIXED L holding point signs should be Red/Black/White - FIXED landscaping near helicenter - FIXED fence coverage near skyport hangar - ADDED trees/bushes to perimeter roadside / Hale Rd - ADDED tree/landscaping assets to field next to runway - ADDED taxiway edge lighting at T taxiway entrance - ADDED reflectors across the taxiways between U and V - ADDED fencing around the long stay carpark and down to the FedEx entry road - ADDED aircraft spawn position for the skyport hangar - ADDED more trees in marked area abeam Taxiway A between T and L The scenery is available on their store for roughly $16.19, requiring at least 4.85 GB of free hard disk space to install. If you enjoy our content and want to support Threshold, consider joining us on Patreon! ‍

entry.title: iniBuilds Updates TriStar Airliner for MSFS 2024
entry.summary: Sim: FS24. iniBuilds has recently issued an update to their rendition of the TriStar Airliner for Microsoft Flight Simulator 2024, delivering a broad range of fixes, adding new features, and more. ‍ ​ Update v1.0.4 addresses critical stability issues that made the airplane unflyable, fixes the spoiler logic, improves the INS with better drift modeling and tracking accuracy, and includes display tweaks. Their PMS has also seen improvements, including dynamic fuel integration, corrected opt alt calculations, and formatting fixes. ​ It also introduces fuel jettison, an en route map path display, several PFB quality-of-life additions, a sync button for the landing performance calculator, and a rudder/tiller toggle option. The complete changelog follows below: V1.0.4SYSTEMS ⚙️ ART⚙️ PFB⚙️ SOUNDS⚙️ ‍ It’s available in the Marketplace for $49.99. If you enjoy our content and want to support Threshold, consider joining us on Patreon! ‍

entry.title: Azrsim Releases Jeddah King Abdulaziz International Airport for MSFS 2024
entry.summary: Sim: FS24. Azrsim has recently released their rendition of Jeddah - King Abdulaziz International Airport (OEJN) for Microsoft Flight Simulator 2024, serving the cities of Jeddah and Mecca in Saudi Arabia, with an average of 53.4 million passengers per year, making it one of the busiest airports in the Middle East. ​ The airport was built as a replacement for Jeddah International Airport in the southern part of the city, a proposal that took over 10 years to become a concrete project, with construction starting in 1975 and reaching completion five years later. In 2006, a new terminal was built as part of a development project, yielding what is considered one of the largest passenger terminals ever built, with 810,000 square meters of built area and the capacity to handle over 30 million passengers annually. It was inaugurated in 2018. It’s a hub for flyadeal, Flynas, and Saudia. Aegean Airlines, Air Arabia, Ariana Afghan Express, Air India Express, Air Cairo, Emirates, Eurowings, flydubai, Indigo, Pegasus Airlines, Turkish Airlines, Wizz Air, and Yemenia also serve it. The scenery features an accurate rendition of the airport, with custom ground textures, high-quality PBR materials, custom night lighting, performance-friendly optimization, custom terminal buildings, and more. It’s available in the iniBuilds Store for roughly $35.23, requiring at least 4.04 GB of free hard disk space to install. If you enjoy our content and want to support Threshold, consider joining us on Patreon! ‍

entry.title: BlueBird Simulations 757 Enters Stage 2 Beta
entry.summary: Sim: FS24. BlueBird Simulations has shared a small update on their highly anticipated Boeing 757 for MSFS, an aircraft years in the making. As of this week, the aircraft has entered stage 2 of its three-phase beta process. This is the largest and most critical testing phase, as almost all major systems are now in the hands of testers. The only major exception is the EFB, which the team says is currently around 50% complete. According to BlueBird, feedback from testers has been good so far, and performance across different systems has reportedly been excellent, which can be a big question mark at this stage of development. Looking ahead, Stage 3 is expected to be a significantly shorter test period. This final phase will primarily focus on weeding out the EFB, alongside various visual refinements across the aircraft. Once the project reaches Stage 3, BlueBird says a release date announcement will follow “a few weeks” later. Also, for now, the developer maintains that the aircraft is still on track for a Q3 release this year, something which they reaffirm directly. Alongside the update, BlueBird has now opened up its Discord server to the public, where people can now engage in conversations. The BlueBird Discord will now act as a more consistent place for updates and answering questions. At release, the base pack will include the 757-200, with both Rolls-Royce and Pratt & Whitney engine options. The 757-300 is planned to follow shortly after as a free update. BlueBird also intends to include both the winglet-equipped and bare wingtip versions of the 757. The passenger 757 is planned to be priced at $80, covering all included variants, and will support both Microsoft Flight Simulator 2020 and Microsoft Flight Simulator 2024. A freighter variant is also in the works, this will be released separately as a paid expansion. That package is said to include variant-specific systems and modelling, and system improvements introduced alongside it will be provided free of charge to existing owners of the base package. BlueBird confirms that an Xbox release is planned, though it will arrive later than the PC version. Future avionics upgrades are also being considered, and the team has plans to recreate the flat panel display system (FPDS) at some point after release, but there are currently no plans to develop the large display system (LDS). Neither will be available at launch. The initial release will include a limited failures implementation, with a more comprehensive failures system planned post-release. A full feature list for the aircraft is expected to be published in June, and BlueBird promises to continuously update the aircraft with new features after release. The initial release is native for MSFS 2020, but will also run in MSFS 2024. Thus, the 2024 version at launch will not include native 2024 features such as interactive exterior panels or walkaround mode support. A fully native MSFS 2024 version is planned and will be released as a free update. Read the full update post on Reddit. ‍ ‍ If you enjoy our content and want to support Threshold, consider joining us on Patreon!

entry.title: FSDG Releases Tulum International Airport for MSFS 2020/2024
entry.summary: Sim: FS24. Flight Sim Development Group has released their latest scenery for Microsoft Flight Simulator, Mexico’s new Tulum International Airport, for both MSFS 2020 and MSFS 2024. Known as Felipe Carrillo Puerto International Airport (MMTL), Tulum is one of Mexico’s newest airports, having opened in late 2023 to serve the rapidly growing Riviera Maya region, as an alternative to Cancun. Unlike Mexico’s larger airports along the coast, Tulum is situated deep into the remote Yucatán jungle. The scenery includes a full recreation of the airport’s terminal and infrastructure, with FSDG promising an accurate and detailed rendition of the real-world layout. Also included is updated aerial imagery, detailed airport modelling, realistic lighting, reflective textures and more. The airport has been optimised for performance. Features: FSDG’s Tulum is available now through the FSDG store for approximately $23.39 USD including VAT. ‍ If you enjoy our content and want to support Threshold, consider joining us on Patreon!

entry.title: A2A Simulations Updates Aerostar 600 for MSFS 2020/2024
entry.summary: Sim: FS24. A2A Simulations has recently issued an update to their Accu-Sim Aerostar 600 for Microsoft Flight Simulator 2020 and 2024, bringing a range of fixes and improvements across the board. Update v1.0.6 improves shakes and turbulence (VR and flat screen), adds single GTN 650/650Xi panel support, GNS brightness adjustments, multiple systems and visual fixes, and much more. The complete changelog for both platforms follows below: Highlights: • Improved shakes & turbulence (VR + 2D) • Single GTN650 / GTN650Xi panel support • GPSS disconnect potential fix • GNS brightness adjustments • Flaps sync with MSFS • Various system & visual fixes ‍ MSFS 2024: ‍ • Added aux hydraulic switch bindings ‍ MSFS 2020: • Fixed rain/ice windshield effects • EDM pop-up enabled It’s available in their store for $49.99. If you enjoy our content and want to support Threshold, consider joining us on Patreon! ‍

entry.title: Aerosoft Updates Milan Malpensa Airport for MSFS 2024
entry.summary: Sim: FS24. Aerosoft has recently released a native update for their Milan Malpensa Airport (LIMC) rendition for Microsoft Flight Simulator 2024, taking full advantage of the new simulator’s capabilities. Update 1.6.0 adds native compatibility with Microsoft Flight Simulator 2024, updates the ground layout to its current state as of 2026, introduces an official GSX profile, and delivers a wealth of improvements and fixes throughout. The complete changelog follows below: Official GSX profile by Luca Verzura, featuring: ‍ The scenery is available on the Aerosoft Shop for roughly $22.99, requiring at least 6 GB of free hard disk space to install. If you enjoy our content and want to support Threshold, consider joining us on Patreon! ‍

entry.title: Digital Aeronautics Releases Mi-2 Hoplite for MSFS 2020
entry.summary: Sim: MSFS20. Digital Aeronautics has released the Mi-2 Hoplite for Microsoft Flight Simulator 2020, a Polish-built Soviet-era utility helicopter, and the developer’s first major aircraft release. The Mi-2, known for a wide range of real-world roles and a quite distinctive design, has here been recreated in great detail. Digital Aeronautics says the aircraft has been built using photo-scanning, photogrammetry, and thousands of real-world reference images, with detailed interior and exterior modelling, high-resolution textures, and a fully clickable cockpit. The developer has modelled a broad range of custom systems, including electrical, fuel, hydraulic, oil, pneumatic, navigation, radio, fire extinguishing, heating, and anti-icing systems. The flight model has also been built around flight manual data and real Mi-2 pilot feedback, with additional custom work in rotor blade behaviour, vibrations, centre-of-gravity effects, signal rockets, aerobatic smoke, rain effects, and cockpit control animations. The helicopter also includes a full VR implementation, support for VR controller-only operation, a comprehensive manual with real-world procedures and checklists, and a Wwise sound package recorded from an actual Mi-2 helicopter. ‍ PRODUCT FEATURES: CUSTOM FEATURES THAT REPEAT THE ACTUAL BEHAVIOR OF A REAL-LIFE HELICOPTER 1) Helicopter systems 2) Engines control 3) Helicopter control 3) Navigation 4) Radio 5) Instruments 6) Lighting equipment 7) Heating and anti-icing systems 8) Atmospheric precipitation (rain) 9) Firing signal rockets and smoke generator 10) Flight Model 11) Interacting with the cockpit and helicopter controls 12) Switching from asymmetric to straight airflow and vice versa 13) Natural deflection of the blades Interactive cockpit features and options 1. VHF allows you to adjust ATC frequencies in multiples of 0.25. 2. Manipulators: combine two modes of interaction. a) mouse wheel - instantaneous state switching; b) left mouse button + movement - realistic state switching. 3. VR - fully adapted, including control of the longitudinal and transverse control knob by moving the controller. 4. The model includes the ability to automatically start and turn off the helicopter with the preparation of systems in the correct sequence through simulator commands. 5. To facilitate control, some controls are connected to simulator commands (see manual list). 6. When controlling the watch, it is necessary to familiarize yourself with the instructions, since due to the complexity of the implementation, combined control of the manipulators was used. 7. Due to the realistic implementation of the electrical system and the load on the batteries at the time of starting, the battery charge is enough for 5-6 unsuccessful starts. Due to the fact that it is not possible to replace the battery, the model must be restarted to reset the state of the batteries. 8. The altimeter has realistic control of the pressure setting handle, which has a reverse direction of rotation (rotation to the right reduces the barometric height, while rotation to the left increases it). 9. To remove the helicopter from the parking brake, press the latch on the helicopter control stick or use the simulator command. 10. The nose steering wheel is made in accordance with a realistic model, and is free castoring (not directly related to the position of the pedals). 11. Due to the realism of helicopter centering, restrictions on helicopter centering and loading should be complied with. 12. For correct display of the helicopter heading on the UGR-4UK device in 3.5 min after starting the helicopter and starting all systems, it is necessary to slave the heading system by pressing the «СОГЛАСОВАНО» button. 13. For toggle switches with more than 3 positions, to move the toggle switch in the required direction, use manipulator located in the direction of toggle switch movement (wiper toggle switch, АКК/ГЕНЕРАТОР toggle switch). 14. Due to the custom implementation of all systems, we do not recommend using default simulator failures. Audio and Immersion ‍ View product page The current release is made specifically for MSFS 2020 and is not compatible with MSFS 2024, due to the aircraft’s custom features. Digital Aeronautics says a dedicated MSFS 2024 version will follow later, then built specifically for the newer simulator. The Mi-2 Hoplite is available exclusively through SimMarket. For the first two weeks after release, it is priced at approximately $29 USD before tax, representing a 30% launch discount. It will eventually settle at a final price of $41.05 USD once further updates have been added. ‍

entry.title: AzurPoly Reveals Tracker S-2FT as Next Aircraft Project
entry.summary: Sim: FS24. AzurPoly has revealed its next aircraft project, with the developer now working on a Tracker S-2FT. The aircraft has already been in development for several months, handled by a new dedicated team within AzurPoly. According to the developer, this means work on the Tracker S-2FT is not affecting its other ongoing projects. Originally designed in the 1950s, the Tracker began its life as a military aircraft but later developed a long second career in aerial firefighting and became a regular sight in wildfire operations across several countries. AzurPoly is specifically developing the S-2FT, a modernised version operated by the French Sécurité Civile. Unlike the original piston-powered Tracker, the S-2FT comes with PT6 turboprops for better performance, reliability, and efficiency during demanding firefighting work close to the ground. For the project, AzurPoly is working with SOS Tracker 07, an association of former firefighters that saved a real Tracker from being scrapped. The aircraft is now preserved at Aubenas Airport in France, giving the team direct access to a real-world airframe during development. AzurPoly has shared a first render of the model, with more information expected later in the development process. The developer has not yet shared a release window, platform details, or feature list for the aircraft. View the full announcement here. ‍ If you enjoy our content and want to support Threshold, consider joining us on Patreon!

entry.title: Nimbus Updates Charlotte Douglas for X-Plane 12 to V1.4
entry.summary: Sim: X-Plane. Nimbus Simulation Studios has released a new V1.4 update for their Charlotte Douglas International Airport (CLT) scenery, bringing a varied list of changes and improvements to the XP12 airport. Charlotte Douglas International Airport serves Charlotte, North Carolina, and sits roughly 9.7 km west of the city’s central business district. What started as a local municipal airport has grown into one of the busiest airports in the world, now serving as a major American Airlines hub on the US East Coast. According to Airports Council International’s 2023 rankings, CLT was the seventh busiest airport worldwide by arrivals and departures, with nonstop service to more than 186 destinations. Nimbus’ rendition of CLT recreates the field with an ultra-high-resolution airport environment, terminal buildings with 3D interiors and crowds, the new control tower completed in early 2022, and extensive night lighting. The scenery includes 4K PBR textures, detailed ground markings, PBR ground textures, high-quality airport vehicles, full parking lots, native XP12 trees, and native X-Plane ground traffic. The airport also features native XP12 jetways with custom models, detailed ground textures, water and snow effects, taxi routes for AI traffic, and low consumption of LIT textures, saving VRAM. The V1.4 update is available now as a free update through the X.Plane.org store. ‍ If you enjoy our content and want to support Threshold, consider joining us on Patreon!

entry.title: FlightSimExpo 2026 Tickets Increase on May 1, Five New Exhibitors Confirmed
entry.summary: Sim: OTHER. Flight Simulation Association has today put out a press release reminding potential attendees that ticket prices for FlightSimExpo 2026 will increase on May 1, with three-day access currently starting at $68 for FSA Captains. ‍ Tickets can be found here. The event will take place from June 12 to June 14, 2026, at the Saint Paul RiverCentre in Minnesota, showcasing more than 60 confirmed exhibitors from across the flight simulation space. These exhibitors were announced last week. FlightSimExpo has for 2026 confirmed a large variety of hardware makers, software developers, training organisations, virtual airlines, media creators, and community groups for this year’s show. Since the initial exhibitor reveal on April 25, five more exhibitors have joined the lineup. These are Flying Art, Gadgetly, Jurassic Jets, National STOL, in partnership with MOZA, and X-Plane. Exhibitor registration for FlightSimExpo 2026 will remain open until May 29, so more names may still be added before the event. National STOL’s partnership with MOZA will host an interactive eSTOL competition on the show floor. According to National STOL Founder Tom Wolf, attendees will be able to compete against other eSTOL competitors and real-world short takeoff and landing pilots in the simulator, with workshops and educational content also planned. National STOL is a U.S.-based Short Take Off and Landing competition series, founded in 2020, where pilots compete to take off and land in the shortest possible distance. The series has grown quickly, turning STOL into a spectator-friendly general aviation competition, with events held across the U.S. FlightSimExpo 2026 will also feature excursions, tours and experiences outside the main exhibit hall. You can now book optional planespotting trips, flight sim classes, aviation tours, and social events through the FlightSimExpo website. These run alongside the main show, which includes developer announcements, hands-on exhibits, panel discussions, and community-led seminars. Book FlightSimExpo 2026 Tours and Experiences here. Friday, June 12, is #FSExpoFriday, the traditional “developer announcement day” of the show, with the main product announcement schedule beginning at 1700Z, or 12 pm CDT. More than 30 announcements are expected, including presentations and video reveals from names such as Thrustmaster, SayIntentions.AI, Combat Pilot, Navigraph, Virtual Fly, X-Plane, SoFly, Meridian GMT, and Microsoft Flight Simulator. After the #FSExpoFriday announcements, the exhibit hall will open for an evening preview from 4:30 pm to 7 pm CDT. Saturday and Sunday will feature the full exhibit hall, seminars, workshops, and other scheduled activities. Hotel availability is now limited, with the primary hotel sold out, and remaining discounted rooms are available starting at $155 per night, plus tax. Airfare discounts are available through Delta, Southwest, United, and codeshare partners. Book hotels here. FlightSimExpo 2026 takes place from June 12 to June 14 at the Saint Paul RiverCentre in Saint Paul, Minnesota. Tickets are available now, with prices increasing by $10 on May 1. Get tickets for FlightSimExpo 2026 here. ‍ If you enjoy our content and want to support Threshold, consider joining us on Patreon!

entry.title: Skydom Teases Al Maktoum International Airport For X-Plane 12
entry.summary: Sim: X-Plane. Skydom have released a teaser of their upcoming Dubai Al Maktoum International Airport for X-Plane 12 on their YouTube channel. The Al Maktoum International Airport (DWC/OMDW), also known as Dubai World Central, is located roughly 35km south of Dubai, and serves the city of Dubai. With construction work on the runway beginning in 2007, the new airport was planned to serve as a replacement for the ageing Dubai International Airport (DXB). Just a couple of years later, in 2010, the Al Maktoum was officially opened, although only for cargo flights. In 2013, the airport was finally opened for passenger airlines, with Flynas and Wizzair being the first airlines to serve DWC. Today, the airport is primarily used by cargo airlines and business jets, with only a handful of airlines serving Al Maktoum year-round. However, that may change in the future, as in 2024, Emirates announced that a major expansion of the airport will take place soon. A completely new passenger terminal is planned, along with the construction of 4 more runways. Once finalised, the Dubai World Central is projected to be the largest airport in the world, being almost five times larger than the DXB. Currently, no information about the pricing or the release date of the scenery has been released, but as always, Threshold will keep you updated! If you enjoy our content and want to support Threshold, consider joining us on Patreon!

entry.title: Microsoft Releases Sim Update 5 for MSFS 2024
entry.summary: Sim: FS24. Microsoft has released Sim Update 5 for MSFS 2024, one of the biggest post-release updates to the simulator so far. The update is available now as version 1.7.27.0 on all platforms and includes PS VR2 support for PlayStation 5 and PlayStation 5 Pro, a long list of performance improvements, several Career mode changes, new avionics options, a weather radar API, and a gigantic bug fix list. ‍ MSFS now supports PS VR2 support for PlayStation 5 and PlayStation 5 Pro. The feature is included as a free update for MSFS 2024 owners on PlayStation and brings the simulator’s VR mode to Sony’s current headset. Microsoft has also added VR-specific graphics settings on PlayStation, improved cockpit interaction with VR controllers, and added an option to hide the HUD in VR while keeping it visible during normal 2D flying. Also related to VR, tooltips will now appear closer to the switch or control being looked at, which should make cockpit interaction feel less detached, and the EFB will now save its VR and non-VR position separately. This means that moving or resizing it in VR should no longer mess with its layout on a normal monitor. Microsoft has also fixed several VR crashes, input issues, toolbar problems, and controller interaction bugs. ‍ ‍ Microsoft says Sim Update 5 improves frame rates and reduces stuttering, notably in large cities and at complex airports. The release notes mention wide ranging optimisation work across building generation, airport parking lots, particle effects, NPC aircraft, cockpit code, texture handling, forest areas, boat and bridge systems, and renderer culling. Microsoft has also fixed a visual glitch affecting AMD Radeon RX 9xxx series cards, along with a crash linked to Frame Generation and smart docking being enabled at the same time. ‍ ‍ The very often requested weather radar API is also now said to be included. Microsoft has now added platform support for more accurate weather radars, finally including tilt control. MSFS developers now have access to new MapView API functions for radar tilt, stabilisation, pitch limits, bank limits, and scan rate in the SDK. This should allow a better base for building proper weather radar systems, instead of having to work around platform limitations. ‍ Heavy and Oversized certifications are now available, and VTOL certification has been unlocked. Microsoft has worked through a lot of mission logic, with better checks for runway length, airport lighting, parking stand size, and blocked spawning positions. The sim should also now be less likely to send players on night missions to airports without lights, or spawn them on stands that are too small or blocked by scenery. Search & Rescue and Banner pickup areas have also been adjusted using vegetation data, which should help avoid missions where the objective is buried in trees or other clutter. ‍ ‍ As for the in-sim EFB, Sim Update 5 has added overload warnings to mass and balance, and now hides EFB apps that are not compatible with the loaded aircraft. Liquid tank naming issues have been fixed, and the handling of flight plan discontinuities and descent paths has been further improved. Fuel planning has also been improved, with warnings for insufficient trip, alternate, or block fuel, as well as automatic final reserve fuel calculations based on ICAO, FAA, or EASA norms. ‍ ‍ The classic Cessna 172 now has new variants equipped with Avidyne IFD540 and IFD550 avionics, a more modern option compared to the GNS. Garmin avionics have also been updated, including the G1000 NXi, G3000, G5000, and G3X Touch, now with better heliport handling and improved VNAV path logic. Further fixes to the 737 MAX 8, 747 family and 787-10 have been made, including autothrottle behaviour, glideslope tracking, LNAV, cabin lighting, FMC behaviour, autopilot, VNAV, cargo, door, sound, LOD, FMC backlighting, seatbelt chime behaviour and more. The C-17 Globemaster now supports night vision, has a walkable ramp and cargobay in walkaround mode, tactical descents, and now supports the selection of reversers in-flight. TACAN has been improved, FMC performance pages enabled, and the stock MSFS system has been implemented for fly-by-wire implementation. The autopilot, HUD, lighting, sounds, and avionics have all been performance-tuned. Airbus aircraft, including the A320neo, A321, A330, A400M, A310, Beluga XL, and H225, have gotten many fixes tied to Career mode, mission skips, terrain display, cargo behaviour, flap logic, aircraft state, and cockpit systems. ‍ Sim Update 5 has fixed some water crack issues, waving bridges in photogrammetry areas, airport lighting and terraforming issues, missing airport buildings, wrong ICAO codes, and several helipad problems. Multiplayer model matching has also been improved, and offline AI traffic is now available again as an experimental feature, which also opens the door for traffic add-ons. A fix for heavy stutters when unplugging or reconnecting USB devices during gameplay has also been made. Microsoft has then added native peripheral support on PC and Xbox, updated the VR toolbar bindings, and added more relative axis controls for the DualSense controller. ‍ ‍ There are better profiling tools, a new Camera API for SimConnect and WASM, more WASM stability fixes, new scenery editor options, and expanded support for Blender and 3ds Max exporters. There’s now support for Blender 4.5, faster export times on large scenes, improved material animation export, and a long list of fixes for lights, LODs, skinned meshes, and materials. You can read the full changelog for Sim Update 5 here. ‍ ‍ Aircraft and Avionics 04 Update will be released on May 5. This separate will add MSFS 2024 features to the Antonov An-2, Expert Series 01 ATR 42-600/72-600, Fokker F.VII, Latecoere 631, and Savoia-Marchetti S.55/S.55X. Existing owners of these aircraft will receive the upgrade for free in MSFS 2024. ‍ Sim Update 5 is available now as a free update for MSFS 2024 owners. ‍ If you enjoy our content and want to support Threshold, consider joining us on Patreon!

entry.title: MM Simulations Releases Appleton International Airport for MSFS 2020/2024
entry.summary: Sim: FS24. MM Simulations has released Appleton International Airport (KATW) for MSFS 2020 and 2024. Appleton International Airport has been modelled with a detailed terminal, including interiors, and an accurate apron and taxiway layout. MM Simulations has also included custom airport buildings, hangars, real-world ground textures, and airport markings. Appleton International Airport is available now through SimMarket, iniBuilds, Contrail, Orbx, flightsim.to, Aerosoft, and SimAtlas (the subscription service), with a Microsoft Flight Simulator Marketplace release also planned soon. You can get it for $22.26, depending on exchange rates. ‍ If you enjoy our content and want to support Threshold, consider joining us on Patreon!

entry.title: A look back at FlightSimWeekend 2026: Video
entry.summary: Sim: OTHER. We have put together a video recap from FlightSimWeekend 2026, filmed at the Aviodrome Aviation Museum in Lelystad, the Netherlands. The video follows our time at the event, taking a look around the exhibition halls, museum areas, developer stands, presentations, and everything in between from one of Europe’s biggest flight simulation gatherings. ‍ Watch it here: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=FTEuni6aFLo ‍ If you enjoy our content and want to support Threshold, consider joining us on Patreon!
